reasoning: San Marino is an extremely safe European microstate with a baseline of zero ACLED-recorded fatalities over the preceding 360 days. ACLED only tracks political violence, such as deaths from battles, explosions, violence against civilians by armed groups, riots, and protests. An extensive search of news from January to March 2026 revealed no protests resulting in violent clashes or fatalities. The largest protest in a decade occurred in early 2026 regarding tax reform, but it was entirely peaceful and concluded without any deaths. There are no ongoing armed conflicts, terror threats, or militant groups operating in San Marino. Since 23 out of the 30 days in the resolution window have already passed with no reported incidents, and the probability of a qualifying fatal event in the remaining week is infinitesimally small, the probability of 1 or more fatalities is squarely at the minimum threshold of 2%.
